title: Creamy Filipino Tapioca Pearls Ginataang Bilo Bilo
description: A traditional Filipino dessert featuring tapioca pearls and coconut milk, flavored with cinnamon and a touch of smokiness. This dish is a nostalgic treat for many Filipinos.
instructions: Rinse the tapioca pearls and soak them in cold water for at least 4 hours or overnight.
Drain and rinse the soaked tapioca pearls, then set them aside.
In a large pot, combine the coconut milk, sugar, water, salt, and smoked sea salt, and heat over medium heat, stirring occasionally.
Add the cinnamon stick and pandan leaves to the pot and continue to heat, stirring occasionally, until the mixture starts to simmer.
Add the soaked tapioca pearls to the pot and cook, stirring occasionally, until they are translucent and the mixture has thickened.
Remove the pot from the heat and let it cool to room temperature.
Cover the pot and refrigerate the mixture for at least 2 hours or overnight to allow it to chill and set.
Before serving, remove the cinnamon stick and pandan leaves from the pot and discard them.
Serve the ginataang bilo bilo chilled, garnished with additional pandan leaves if desired.
